title: VTEX IO Store Framework — blocks (auto-gerado da doc oficial)
description: Autocomplete e validacao de props de 370 blocos VTEX IO.

paymentMethods.description: List of supported payment method icons to display.

showInColor.description: Whether the payment method icons are displayed in color or not.

^accepted-payment-methods(#.*)?$.description: Block that displays accepted payment method icons in the store footer.

onClickBehavior.description: Controls what happens when users click the button (redirect to product page, add to cart, ensure SKU selection, or trigger shipping modal).

onClickEventPropagation.description: Controls whether the onClick event triggered upon user clicks should propagate to parent elements of the page.

isOneClickBuy.description: Determines whether the user should be redirected to the checkout page (true) or remain on the current page (false) when the button is clicked.

customOneClickBuyLink.description: Defines the link to which users will be redirected when the button is clicked and isOneClickBuy is set to true.

customToastUrl.description: Defines the link to which users will be redirected when the toast notification (shown after adding an item to the minicart) is clicked.

text.description: Defines a custom text message to be displayed on the Add to Cart button (automatic translation follows store default language).

unavailableText.description: Defines a custom text message to be displayed on the button when a product is unavailable (automatic translation follows store default language).

customPixelEventId.description: Defines the id for the event that the button will send upon user interaction.

^add-to-cart-button(#.*)?$.description: Button component that adds items to the cart, with configurable click behavior, one-click buy, and toast notification options.

toastURL.description: Changes the link of the toast message shown after adding a product to the wishlist.

^add-to-list-btn(#.*)?$.description: Button that adds a product to the wishlist, rendering a heart icon on product shelves and product pages.

^addon-details-btn(#.*)?$.description: Extension point block that accepts any component as a child within the product-add-to-list-button. Defined as a wildcard interface ("component": "*") with no configurable props of its own.

^addon-summary-btn(#.*)?$.description: Extension point block in vtex.product-summary that acts as a slot/placeholder for addon buttons (such as add-to-list from vtex.wish-list). It is defined with component "*" in interfaces.json, meaning it accepts any component. No configurable props are declared at the base block l

optionsDisplay.description: Defines whether a checkbox (box) or a dropdown list (select) will be used to display the attachment pre-defined options.

initiallyOpened.description: Controls the default open/collapsed state of the customization box: 'always' keeps it open, 'required' only opens it when the attachment is required.

^assembly-option-input-values(#.*)?$.description: Renders the input values for an assembly option attachment on the product details page, supporting predefined options (checkbox or dropdown), free text, and boolean inputs.

^assembly-option-item-children-descripti.description: Displays a summary with all selected attachments. No configurable props are documented for this block in the official VTEX IO product-customizer documentation (https://developers.vtex.com/docs/apps/vtex.product-customizer and https://github.com/vtex-apps/product-customizer).

buttonProps.description: Define o comportamento do botão Customize, incluindo seu posicionamento e os blocos filhos que compõem o conteúdo do modal aberto ao clicar no botão.

^assembly-option-item-customize(#.*)?$.description: Bloco que renderiza um item de opção de montagem com um botão "Customize" que abre um modal para customização do produto.

^assembly-option-item-image(#.*)?$.description: Displays the attachment/assembly option item image. Reads image URL and name from context; has no configurable props.

^assembly-option-item-name(#.*)?$.description: Displays the assembly option item's attachment name. It is a pure display component that reads the name from the ProductAssemblyContext and has no configurable props.

^assembly-option-item-price(#.*)?$.description: Displays the attachment/assembly option item price. No configurable props are documented or exposed — all data is derived from the ProductAssemblyContext.

^assembly-option-item-quantity-selector(.description: Renders a quantity selector for an assembly option item. The component is fully driven by React context (useProductAssemblyItem, useProductAssemblyGroupState, useProductAssemblyGroupDispatch) and exposes no configurable props in the official documentation or source code.

attemptPageTypeSearch.description: Uses the input term to try to navigate to the proper page type (e.g. a department, a brand, a category) if true.

classes.description: Overrides default CSS handles for custom styling.

closeMenu.description: Function that closes the autocomplete dropdown menu (Downshift-related).

customSearchPageUrl.description: Template for a custom URL; accepts ${term} as placeholder to interpolate the searched term.

data.description: GraphQL data response containing the autocomplete results.

highlightedIndex.description: The index of the item that should be highlighted in the autocomplete list (Downshift-related).

getItemProps.description: Returns the props for every menu item element being rendered (Downshift-related).

getMenuProps.description: Returns the props for the ul or root element of the autocomplete menu (Downshift-related).

inputValue.description: The current search query text typed by the user.

onClearInput.description: Function that clears the search input field.

isOpen.description: Determines whether the autocomplete dropdown menu should be displayed.

^autocomplete-result-list(#.*)?$.description: Renders the autocomplete result list dropdown for the search bar, integrating with Downshift to display suggested search results as the user types.

maxTopSearches.description: Maximum number of terms in the top searches list.

maxHistory.description: Maximum number of terms in the search history list.

autocompleteWidth.description: Autocomplete width as a number between 0 and 100.

productLayout.description: Defines the product layout in the suggested products list.

hideTitles.description: If true, all section titles will be hidden.

historyFirst.description: If true, the Search History list will be prioritized over other lists.

customBreakpoints.description: Specifies the maximum number of suggested products per responsive breakpoint.

simulationBehavior.description: Use 'skip' for faster searches without fetching latest prices and promotions.

customPage.description: Defines a custom page to be used for autocomplete links.

orderBy.description: Controls the sorting order of suggested products. Invalid values default to relevance-based sorting.

^autocomplete-result-list\\.v2(#.*)?$.description: Renders the autocomplete result list with top searches, search history, suggested terms, and suggested products.

^availability-badges(#.*)?$.description: Displays product-summary badges driven by delivery promise data. Has no content schema; relies on ProductSummaryContext and useDeliveryPromiseState for delivery information.

^availability-subscriber(#.*)?$.description: Displays a form for customers to subscribe to availability notifications for out-of-stock products. Has no configurable props.

display.description: Determines how the component renders: as a text label button or as a caret icon only.

displayThreshold.description: Vertical scroll position in pixels at which the button becomes visible.

^back-to-top-button(#.*)?$.description: A button that redirects users to the top of the page when clicked. Can be rendered as a text button or a caret icon, and becomes visible after a configurable scroll threshold.

showOnMobile.description: Determines whether Breadcrumb should also be displayed on mobile.

homeIconSize.description: Controls the size property of the IconHome icon.

caretIconSize.description: Controls the size property of the IconCaret icon.

^breadcrumb(#.*)?$.description: Breadcrumb navigation block that shows a user's browsing history up to their current location in the store.

showOnMobile.description: Determines whether the breadcrumb should also be displayed on mobile devices.

homeIconSize.description: Controls the size property of the IconHome icon rendered at the start of the breadcrumb.

caretIconSize.description: Controls the size property of the IconCaret separator icon rendered between breadcrumb items.

^breadcrumb\\.product(#.*)?$.description: Breadcrumb block for product detail pages, displaying the navigation path based on the product's category tree.

showOnMobile.description: It determines whether Breadcrumb should also be displayed on mobile.

homeIconSize.description: Controls the size property of IconHome.

caretIconSize.description: Controls the size property of IconCaret.

^breadcrumb\\.search(#.*)?$.description: Breadcrumb block specific for search result pages. Renders breadcrumb navigation on search result pages.

available.description: Whether the Buy Button block is available for using (true) or not (false).

customToastURL.description: Defines the link to where users will be redirected when the Buy Button is clicked and isOneClickBuy is set to true.

isOneClickBuy.description: Controls whether users proceed directly to checkout (true) or remain on the current page (false) after clicking the button.

large.description: Whether the Buy Button should fill its whole width (true) or not (false).

shouldOpenMinicart.description: Whether the Minicart should be opened when the Buy Button is clicked (true) or not (false).

shouldAddToCart.description: Whether the Buy Button should add products to the minicart when clicked (true) or not (false).

showItemsPrice.description: Whether the total price of items added to the cart should be displayed (true) or not (false).

showToast.description: Whether a Toast (pop-up notification) should be displayed when adding an item to the minicart (true) or not (false).

showTooltipOnSkuNotSelected.description: Whether a tooltip should be displayed when the Buy Button is clicked with no SKU selected (true) or not (false).

^buy-button(#.*)?$.description: Bloco de botão de compra do vtex.store-components, usado para adicionar produtos ao carrinho ou redirecionar para o checkout.

autoplaySpeed.description: Set the automatic banner transition interval (in seconds)

height.description: Sets the banners height in pixels

banners.description: Array of banner objects to be displayed in the carousel

^carousel(#.*)?$.description: Renders a carousel of banners with automatic or manual transitions, dots, and arrows navigation.

showAllDepartments.description: Shows all department categories in the menu.

menuDisposition.description: Indicates the menu's position on the screen.

showSubcategories.description: Defines if the subcategories will be displayed.

departments.description: List of department items (with id) to be displayed on the menu.

mobileMode.description: Renders the category menu in a sidebar if set to true.

^category-menu(#.*)?$.description: Renders a category navigation menu with support for departments, subcategories, and mobile sidebar mode.

title.description: Display title shown at the top of the summary block.

^checkout-summary(#.*)?$.description: Displays the order totalizers and allows the user to add coupon codes. Works within Minicart and Checkout Cart components.

totalizers.description: Array of totalizer objects ({ id: string; value: number | null; name: string }) to be displayed in the summary.

totalizersToShow.description: Array of totalizer IDs that should be displayed inside the compact summary component.

total.description: Total value of the order form to be displayed at the bottom of the summary.

totalCalculation.description: Controls how the Total shown at the bottom of the summary is calculated.

^checkout-summary\\.compact(#.*)?$.description: Compact version of the checkout summary block, displaying order totalizers in a condensed format for use within Minicart or similar interfaces.

conditions.description: List of condition objects to be evaluated; each object defines subject, arguments (v2) or verb/object (v1), and toBe.

matchType.description: Determines how many conditions must be satisfied for the layout to render: all, any, or none.

Then.description: Block ID to render when the conditions are satisfied; if not provided, child blocks are rendered instead.

Else.description: Block ID to render when the conditions are not satisfied.

^condition-layout(#.*)?$.description: Renders child blocks conditionally based on a set of evaluated conditions within a context (product, binding, category, telemarketing). Never used directly — always via a context variant such as condition-layout.product.

conditions.description: List of desired conditions to be evaluated for the binding context.

matchType.description: Layout rendering criteria: all conditions must match, any one must match, or none must match.

Then.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are met.

Else.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are not met.

^condition-layout\\.binding(#.*)?$.description: Renders blocks conditionally based on store binding conditions. Evaluates one or more binding-related conditions and renders the appropriate child block.

conditions.description: List of desired conditions to be evaluated for rendering.

matchType.description: Rendering criteria: 'all' requires all conditions, 'any' requires at least one, 'none' requires no conditions to match.

Then.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are met.

Else.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are not met.

^condition-layout\\.category(#.*)?$.description: Block that conditionally renders content based on category or department conditions in the current navigation context.

conditions.description: List of desired conditions to be evaluated for conditional rendering.

matchType.description: Layout rendering criteria: all (all conditions must be met), any (at least one must be met), or none (no conditions must be met).

Then.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are met.

Else.description: Name of the block to be rendered if the conditions are not met.

^condition-layout\\.product(#.*)?$.description: Renders blocks conditionally based on product-related conditions (product ID, category, brand, availability, etc.). Evaluates a list of conditions and renders the Then or Else block accordingly.

conditions.description: List of desired conditions to be evaluated for rendering.

matchType.description: Layout rendering criteria: all (all conditions must be met), any (at least one must be met), or none (no condition must be met).

Then.description: Name of the block to be rendered when the conditions are met. If not defined, children blocks are rendered instead.

Else.description: Name of the block to be rendered when the conditions are not met.

^condition-layout\\.telemarketing(#.*)?$.description: Block that defines rendering conditions based on the telemarketing (impersonation) context, displaying different child blocks depending on whether the conditions are met.

^did-you-mean(#.*)?$.description: Component that suggests a possible misspelling correction for the current search query. It has no configurable props — all data is retrieved internally via the SearchPageContext and a GraphQL correction query.

blockClass.description: Block ID defined by you that will be used in CSS customizations.

^disclosure-content(#.*)?$.description: Block that declares the content to be revealed when the disclosure trigger is activated.

initialVisibility.description: Controls whether the content is displayed (visible) or hidden by default when the page loads.

animated.description: When true, enables animations and adds data-* attributes to the content element for CSS-driven transitions.

^disclosure-layout(#.*)?$.description: Layout block that shows or hides child content based on user interaction (disclosure/accordion pattern).

maxVisible.description: Defines how many disclosure-layout blocks can have their content displayed at a time: one (only one open at a time) or many (multiple can be open simultaneously).

^disclosure-layout-group(#.*)?$.description: Groups multiple disclosure-layout blocks and controls how many can be open simultaneously.

Show.description: Name of the block that will be rendered when prompted to show the content.

Hide.description: Name of the block that will be rendered when prompted to hide the content.

^disclosure-state-indicator(#.*)?$.description: Optional block that renders chevrons or other UI elements that change appearance based on the disclosure open/closed state.

Show.description: Name of the block rendered when the component is in the 'show content' state.

Hide.description: Name of the block rendered when the component is in the 'hide content' state.

as.description: HTML tag to be applied to the component when rendered on the UI.

children.description: Name of the block rendered when no blocks are declared in the Show or Hide props.

blockClass.description: Block identifier used to apply custom CSS classes via the blockClass handle.

^disclosure-trigger(#.*)?$.description: Trigger element for the disclosure-layout. Renders a clickable element that shows or hides the disclosure content. Supports custom HTML tags and separate blocks for show/hide states.

Show.description: Name of the block that will be rendered when prompted to show the content.

Hide.description: Name of the block that will be rendered when prompted to hide the content.

as.description: HTML tag to be applied to the component when rendered on the UI.

children.description: Name of the block that will be rendered if no blocks are declared in the Show or Hide props.

blockClass.description: Block ID defined by you that will be used in CSS customizations.

^disclosure-trigger-group(#.*)?$.description: Wraps multiple disclosure-trigger blocks for coordinated show/hide behavior across a disclosure-layout-group.

maxWidth.description: Defines the maximum width of the open drawer.

isFullWidth.description: Controls whether the open drawer should occupy the full available width.

slideDirection.description: Controls the sliding animation direction when the drawer opens.

backdropMode.description: Controls whether the backdrop should be displayed when the drawer is open.

renderingStrategy.description: Determines whether child content renders on interaction (lazy) or on page load (eager).

customPixelEventId.description: Defines the store event ID responsible for triggering the drawer to automatically open on the interface.

customPixelEventName.description: Store event name (e.g., 'addToCart') that triggers automatic drawer opening.

^drawer(#.*)?$.description: Drawer block from vtex.store-drawer — a sliding panel component that can be triggered by user interaction or store events.

size.description: Defines the size of the icon inside the button.

type.description: Defines the type of the icon.

text.description: Defines the text inside the button. The icon will not be rendered if text is defined.

^drawer-close-button(#.*)?$.description: Button used to close the drawer. Supports icon size, icon type, and optional text label.

^drawer-header(#.*)?$.description: Layout block used to customize the header of the Drawer, which contains the close button. Accepts children blocks (drawer-close-button must be included). No configurable props are documented in the official VTEX IO documentation (https://github.com/vtex-apps/drawer).

customPixelEventId.description: Defines the event ID to be sent whenever users interact with the Drawer component.

^drawer-trigger(#.*)?$.description: Block that wraps the trigger element (e.g. a button or icon) responsible for opening the Drawer component.

categoryFiltersMode.description: Controls whether category filters use href attributes with category URLs (href) or HTML divs with role=link (default).

layout.description: Specifies if the layout is responsive or fixed to desktop.

maxItemsDepartment.description: Maximum number of department items shown before a See more button appears.

maxItemsCategory.description: Maximum number of category items shown before a See more button appears.

initiallyCollapsed.description: Makes filters start in collapsed state (true) or open (false).

openFiltersMode.description: Controls how many filters can be open simultaneously.

filtersTitleHtmlTag.description: HTML tag used for filter titles.

scrollToTop.description: Scroll behavior when a facet is selected.

truncateFilters.description: Shortens filter lists with more than 10 options, adding a See more button.

closeOnOutsideClick.description: Closes the filter navigator when clicking outside it (works with openFiltersMode: one).

appliedFiltersOverview.description: Shows or hides the applied filters overview section.

totalProductsOnMobile.description: Displays or hides the total product count on mobile.

fullWidthOnMobile.description: Uses full screen width for the filter navigator on mobile devices.

navigationTypeOnMobile.description: Mobile navigation style: full page overlay or collapsible panel.

updateOnFilterSelectionOnMobile.description: Updates search results immediately on filter selection on mobile.

drawerDirectionMobile.description: Direction from which the filter drawer opens on mobile.

showClearByFilter.description: Shows a clear button next to individual filter names.

showClearAllFiltersOnDesktop.description: Displays a button to reset all applied filters on desktop.

priceRangeLayout.description: Price range filter display style: slider only or slider with input fields.

facetOrdering.description: Custom sorting rules for filters/facets ordering.

showQuantityBadgeOnMobile.description: Shows a badge indicating the number of active filters on mobile.

^filter-navigator\\.v3(#.*)?$.description: Renders the search filters (facets) navigation panel. Supports responsive layout, collapsible filters, mobile drawer, price range slider, and custom facet ordering.

^flex-layout(#.*)?$.description: Namespace container for the Flex Layout app. Should never be used directly — use flex-layout.row or flex-layout.col instead. Has no configurable props of its own.

blockClass.description: Block container class used as a block identifier for CSS customizations.

borderColor.description: The color of the border applied to the column.

borderWidth.description: Magnitude for applying the bw Tachyons token to the column (values 0 to 5).

border.description: Defines on which sides of the column a border should apply (top, right, bottom, left, or all).

horizontalAlign.description: Controls horizontal alignment for items inside the flex-layout.col.

marginLeft.description: Magnitude for applying the ml Tachyons token to the column (values 0 to 10).

marginRight.description: Magnitude for applying the mr Tachyons token to the column (values 0 to 10).

paddingLeft.description: Magnitude for applying the pl Tachyons token to the column (values 0 to 10).

paddingRight.description: Magnitude for applying the pr Tachyons token to the column (values 0 to 10).

preventVerticalStretch.description: Prevents the column from stretching vertically to fill its parent's height.

rowGap.description: Magnitude for applying the pb Tachyons token to rows inside the flex-layout.col (values 0 to 10).

verticalAlign.description: Controls vertical alignment for items inside the flex-layout.col.

width.description: Sets the width of the column. Accepts a percentage (0-100%) or the value 'grow'.

^flex-layout\\.col(#.*)?$.description: Column block for flex layout, used to organize content vertically within a flex row.

blockClass.description: Block container class used as a block identifier for CSS customizations.

borderColor.description: The color of the border applied to the row.

borderWidth.description: A number magnitude (0–5) for applying the `bw` Tachyons token to the row border width.

border.description: Defines on which sides of the row a border should apply: top, right, bottom, left, or all.

colGap.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `pr` Tachyons token as gap between columns.

colSizing.description: Controls whether columns have equal width or auto sizing.

fullWidth.description: Whether the component occupies all available width from its parent.

horizontalAlign.description: Controls horizontal alignment for row items.

colJustify.description: Controls the space between columns and borders, following the justify-content CSS property.

marginBottom.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `mb` Tachyons token as bottom margin to the row.

marginTop.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `mt` Tachyons token as top margin to the row.

paddingBottom.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `pb` Tachyons token as bottom padding to the row.

paddingTop.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `pt` Tachyons token as top padding to the row.

preserveLayoutOnMobile.description: Whether the row preserves horizontal layout instead of breaking into columns on mobile devices.

preventHorizontalStretch.description: Prevents the row from stretching horizontally to fill the parent width.

preventVerticalStretch.description: Prevents the row from stretching vertically to fill the parent height using items-stretch.

rowGap.description: A number magnitude (0–10) for applying the `pb` Tachyons token as gap between rows in the flex layout.

htmlId.description: Adds an HTML id to the flex row element, enabling anchor link navigation to page sections.

arialabel.description: Aria-label attribute added to the row for accessibility purposes.

^flex-layout\\.row(#.*)?$.description: A layout block that arranges its children in a horizontal row using flexbox, supporting alignment, spacing, borders, and responsive behavior.

showPaymentFormsInColor.description: Display payment method icons in color instead of grayscale.

showSocialNetworksInColor.description: Display social network icons in color instead of grayscale.

showVtexLogoInColor.description: Display the VTEX logo in color instead of grayscale.

^footer(#.*)?$.description: Footer block for VTEX IO stores. Renders address, social networks, payment methods, and custom links. Supports both a legacy layout (via props) and a composable layout using footer-layout child blocks.

^footer-layout(#.*)?$.description: Container block for the store footer layout. Acts as a pure composition wrapper that accepts child blocks (footer-row, social-networks, accepted-payment-methods, powered-by, etc.) with no configurable props of its own.

^footer-layout\\.desktop(#.*)?$.description: Container block for the desktop footer layout. Inherits composition from footer-layout and targets desktop devices. Accepts children blocks but exposes no configurable props of its own.

^footer-layout\\.mobile(#.*)?$.description: Device-specific footer layout container for mobile. It is a variant of footer-layout with device=mobile. The FooterLayout React component accepts only children (PropsWithChildren<{}>) with no configurable first-level props.

fullWidth.description: Define if the row should take the full width of its container.

inverted.description: Define if background and text colors should be inverted.

blockClass.description: Custom CSS class identifier used for styling overrides via CSS handles.

^footer-row(#.*)?$.description: A layout row container for the store footer that renders children with optional full-width and color-inversion settings.

^footer-spacer(#.*)?$.description: Simple deprecated spacer block that renders a flex-grow div to fill available horizontal space inside a footer row. It accepts no configurable props.

entity.description: The Master Data entity where the submitted document will be saved.

schema.description: The JSON schema name used for form validation, as defined in Master Data.

^form(#.*)?$.description: Block that renders a form connected to a VTEX Master Data entity and JSON schema.

pointer.description: JSON schema pointer (path) indicating which schema property the form block inputs should be validated against, e.g. #/properties/address.

uiSchema.description: Redefines how the form-field-group block should render each sub-property declared in the JSON schema path defined in pointer.

^form-field-group(#.*)?$.description: Block that groups form fields based on a JSON schema path, allowing validation and custom rendering of sub-properties.

^form-input(#.*)?$.description: Base container block for form input variants in vtex.store-form. Defined as a no-operation component ("nop") that acts as a namespace for form-input.text, form-input.textarea, form-input.checkbox, form-input.radiogroup, form-input.dropdown, and form-input.upload. Has no configura

pointer.description: JSON schema pointer (path) indicating which schema property the checkbox input should be validated against, e.g. #/properties/agreement.

label.description: Field display name rendered next to the checkbox in the form.

^form-input\\.checkbox(#.*)?$.description: Renders a checkbox input field connected to a Master Data JSON schema field within a VTEX Store Form.

pointer.description: JSON schema pointer, i.e., the JSON schema path (e.g. #/properties/firstName) against which the field input is validated.

label.description: Field name displayed when the dropdown is rendered.

^form-input\\.dropdown(#.*)?$.description: Renders a dropdown (select) field in a VTEX IO store form, connected to a JSON schema for Master Data validation.

pointer.description: Mandatory. JSON schema pointer (path) used to validate the field input, e.g. #/properties/firstName.

label.description: Mandatory. Field display name rendered alongside the radio group.

^form-input\\.radiogroup(#.*)?$.description: Renders a radio button group field connected to a JSON schema pointer for form validation in VTEX IO Store Framework.

pointer.description: JSON schema path (e.g. #/properties/firstName) used to validate the form block input against the schema.

inputType.description: Defines the type of the text field: regular input, hidden (pre-defined value), or password.

value.description: Pre-defined text to be submitted when inputType is set to hidden.

label.description: Label displayed for the input field.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown inside the text input field.

^form-input\\.text(#.*)?$.description: Renders a text input field inside a VTEX IO store form, supporting regular text, hidden, and password input types.

pointer.description: JSON schema pointer (e.g. #/properties/firstName) indicating which schema path this input should be validated against. Mandatory.

label.description: Field name displayed when the textarea is rendered. Mandatory.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown inside the textarea input.

^form-input\\.textarea(#.*)?$.description: Renders a textarea (large text input) field inside a store form, validated against a JSON schema via Master Data.

pointer.description: Mandatory. JSON schema pointer (e.g., #/properties/address) used to validate the form block input.

accept.description: Optional. Customizes the accepted file type format using the pattern *.TYPEFILE. Defaults to images and PDF files.

^form-input\\.upload(#.*)?$.description: Renders an upload field in the form, supporting image and PDF file types by default.

label.description: Text displayed on the submit button.

^form-submit(#.*)?$.description: Renders a button to submit the user form content.

^form-success(#.*)?$.description: Container block that accepts an array of child blocks rendered when the form is successfully submitted. Any children block is valid. No configurable props are documented.

layouts.description: Arranges and displays items on the search results page, supporting multiple layout options with name, component, and itemsPerRow settings.

customSummaryInterval.description: Defines the item interval at which the Gallery should render a custom product-summary block alongside the other items.

CustomSummary.description: Defines a block to render according to the interval defined by customSummaryInterval.

preferredSKU.description: Controls which SKU will be initially selected in the product summary when the product has multiple SKUs.

^gallery(#.*)?$.description: Renders the product list on the search results page, supporting multiple layout configurations and custom product summaries.

name.description: Specifies the name of the layout option. Must match the value passed to the `name` prop in the gallery block's layouts configuration.

^gallery-layout-option(#.*)?$.description: Defines a layout option button rendered inside the gallery-layout-switcher block. The name prop must match the layout name declared in the parent gallery block's layouts prop.

^gallery-layout-switcher(#.*)?$.description: Logical block that allows users to switch between the available gallery layouts. Receives no props and expects gallery-layout-option blocks as children.

^gift-description(#.*)?$.description: Renders the gift's description provided by the product-gift-list block. No configurable props are documented for this block.

imageLabel.description: Label of the image variant that should be rendered; if not set, the first available SKU image is used.

^gift-image(#.*)?$.description: Renders the gift product image inside the product-gifts layout. If no imageLabel is defined, uses the first available image from the product SKU.

linkToProductPage.description: Whether or not the gift-name block should be a link to the gift's product page.

nameType.description: Determines which name displays with the gift: the product name or the SKU name.

^gift-name(#.*)?$.description: Renders the name of a gift product within the Product Gifts app, with options to link to the product page and choose between product name or SKU name.

text.description: Translatable string (ICU pattern) with variables (exceedingItems, totalGifts, visibleItems) used to render gift-related text.

^gift-text(#.*)?$.description: Reads Catalog data regarding the product's gifts and renders a customizable text using ICU pattern with gift-related variables.

^header(#.*)?$.description: Top-level header container block that accepts header-layout.desktop and header-layout.mobile as children. Has no configurable props of its own.

sticky.description: Define se a borda do header deve ser fixa (sticky) no layout. Deprecated: use vtex.sticky-layout no lugar.

^header-border(#.*)?$.description: Adds a 1px margin to a header row. Can behave in a sticky manner (deprecated in favor of vtex.sticky-layout).

classes.description: Overrides default CSS handles. Keys must match the available CSS handles: forceCenter and forceCenterInnerContainer.

^header-force-center(#.*)?$.description: Centralizes its child blocks within a header row. Deprecated — use vtex.sticky-layout and inline elements instead.

^header-layout(#.*)?$.description: Container block that defines the Header layout for desktop and mobile devices through header-row blocks. It has no configurable props of its own.

^header-layout\\.desktop(#.*)?$.description: Defines the Header layout for desktop devices through header-row blocks. This block itself has no documented props — all configuration is done via its header-row children.

^header-layout\\.desktop-scroll(#.*)?$.description: Container block that defines the Header layout for desktop devices when in scroll mode. It is a variant of header-layout.desktop with no configurable props of its own — it acts as a composition host for header-row children blocks.

^header-layout\\.mobile(#.*)?$.description: Defines the Header layout for mobile devices through header-row blocks. This block has no documented first-level props of its own; it acts as a layout container composed entirely of header-row children.

^header-layout\\.mobile-scroll(#.*)?$.description: Device-specific mobile-scroll variant of the header layout container. Defined as an empty interface in store/interfaces.json, it inherits from header-layout with no additional configurable props of its own.

zIndex.description: Controls the vertical stacking order of elements for overlapping.

sticky.description: Determines if the header row remains fixed at the top of the screen during scroll (true) or not (false).

fullWidth.description: Determines if the header row should span the full width of the screen.

inverted.description: If true, the row uses the inverted base color defined in styles.json.

^header-row(#.*)?$.description: Creates Header lines according to store needs, with support for sticky behavior, full-width layout, z-index stacking, and inverted color mode.

^header-spacer(#.*)?$.description: Adds a flexible space between blocks in a header row. Blocks declared before header-spacer are pushed to the left, and blocks declared after are pushed to the right. No configurable props are documented for this block.

^header\\.full(#.*)?$.description: Container block that composes the full store header by nesting header-layout.desktop and header-layout.mobile blocks. It has no configurable props of its own.

^image-new(#.*)?$.description: Renders a single image with link, loading strategy, fetch priority, and optional analytics/promotion tracking. Defined in store/interfaces.json referencing the Image component and app:vtex.store-image#/definitions/Image schema.

images.description: Array of image objects to be rendered in the slider.

height.description: Height in pixels applied to all images in the slider.

sliderLayoutConfig.description: Slider behaviour settings passed to the underlying Slider Layout (itemsPerPage, infinite, showNavigationArrows, showPaginationDots, usePagination).

preload.description: Preloads the first image in the slider, prioritising its display over other assets.

^image-slider(#.*)?$.description: Renders a slider of images using the list-context.image-list content and the Slider Layout engine.

blockClass.description: Extra class name for custom styling

callToActionLinkTarget.description: Controls where the CTA link opens

callToActionText.description: Text displayed inside the call-to-action component

imageActionUrl.description: Redirect URL when image is clicked

isFullModeStyle.description: Uses image as background with overlay text

textAlignment.description: Horizontal text alignment inside the card

textMode.description: Processing mode for headline, subhead, and body text

textPosition.description: Position of the text content relative to the image

fetchpriority.description: Image fetch priority hint (high, low, auto)

preload.description: Prioritizes loading of the first image for display

^info-card(#.*)?$.description: Displays a card with image, headline, subhead, body text, and a call-to-action button or link.

label.description: Link text displayed to the user.

href.description: Link URL. Supports product specification variables and query strings.

scrollTo.description: Element anchor to scroll to after navigation (e.g. #footer).

target.description: Specifies where the linked URL displays, equivalent to the HTML anchor target attribute.

displayMode.description: How the link will be displayed: as an anchor or as a button.

buttonProps.description: Defines how the link button will be displayed. Only used when displayMode is set to button.

escapeLinkRegex.description: Regular expression to remove special characters from product specifications used in the link.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between the current and linked documents for SEO purposes.

^link(#.*)?$.description: Renders a link (anchor or button) in the storefront, supporting internal navigation, scroll anchors, SEO rel attributes, and customizable button appearance.

message.description: Text displayed on the link counter. Supports ICU Message Format with the variable `sellerQuantity` (number of sellers).

^link-seller(#.*)?$.description: Renders a link on the product page that directs users to the Seller Selector page listing all available sellers for the product.

label.description: Link text displayed to the user.

target.description: Specifies where the linked URL opens, matching HTML <a> element behavior.

displayMode.description: Rendering style: anchor (unstyled link) or button (customizable button).

buttonProps.description: Button styling configuration (variant and size) used when displayMode is button.

escapeLinkRegex.description: RegExp pattern to remove special characters from product specs used in the URL.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between the current document and linked ones for better SEO.

^link\\.product(#.*)?$.description: Renders a link inside a product context, supporting anchor and button display modes. Shares all props with the base link block.

^list-context(#.*)?$.description: Base interface block that creates a list context to be extended by other apps (e.g., list-context.product-list, list-context.image-list). It has no configurable props of its own; all customization is done in the extending block or in list-context-renderer.

listElement.description: HTML element used to render the list (e.g. div, ul).

itemElement.description: HTML element used to render each item of the list (e.g. div, li).

^list-context-renderer(#.*)?$.description: Renders a list built via list-context, wrapping list items with configurable HTML elements.

images.description: Array of objects that declares all the images to be rendered.

openNewTab.description: Abre o link em uma nova aba.

width.description: Largura da imagem (px ou valor CSS).

analyticsProperties.description: Envia eventos de analytics ao clicar.

promotionProductId.description: ID do produto associado para analytics.

promotionProductName.description: Nome do produto associado para analytics.

height.description: Image height for all images declared in the image object (in px).

preload.description: Preloads the first image in a list, prioritizing the image display over other assets.

experimentalSetExplicitDimensions.description: Sets explicit width/height attributes for images when provided in pixels.

^list-context\\.image-list(#.*)?$.description: Block that renders a list of images using the List Context provider, allowing composition with other list context blocks like slider-layout.

category.description: Category ID of the listed items. For subcategories, use '/' as separator (e.g. '1/2/3').

specificationFilters.description: Specification filters of the listed items, each with id and value fields.

orderBy.description: Sort order of the listed items.

hideUnavailableItems.description: Hides unavailable items. Treated as true when Delivery Promise is enabled.

maxItems.description: Maximum number of items to be fetched.

skusFilter.description: Controls which SKUs are returned for each product in the query.

installmentCriteria.description: Controls the price displayed when different installment options are available.

listName.description: Name of the list used in Google Analytics events.

preferredSKU.description: Controls which SKU is pre-selected in the product summary.

^list-context\\.product-list(#.*)?$.description: Renders a list of product summaries using list context, supporting filtering, sorting, and SKU control.

products.description: Array of products to be rendered in the static list.

listName.description: Name of the list property sent in Google Analytics events.

preferredSKU.description: Controls which SKU will be initially selected in the product summary.

^list-context\\.product-list-static(#.*).description: Renders a static list of products in the Product Summary component without running a GraphQL query, receiving products directly via props.

showViewEmptyList.description: Shows a custom view if no product has been added to the wishlist.

^list-context\\.wishlist(#.*)?$.description: Provides product data to build the /wishlist page shared with the My Account page.

^locale-switcher(#.*)?$.description: Componente que permite ao usuário trocar o idioma atual da loja. Não expõe props configuráveis — seu comportamento é determinado pelo runtime do VTEX IO (bindings configurados via suporte VTEX).

optionsTitle.description: Text to entitle the login options

emailAndPasswordTitle.description: Text to display the email-and-password option

accessCodeTitle.description: Text to display the access-code option

emailPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the email input

passwordPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the password input

acessCodePlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the access code input

showPasswordVerificationIntoTooltip.description: Whether a tooltip responsible for checking the password format should be shown

showIconProfile.description: Whether the hpa-profile icon should be displayed on the Login component

iconLabel.description: Text string to entitle the Login icon

hideIconLabel.description: Whether the text string defined for the Login icon should be hidden

providerPasswordButtonLabel.description: Text to be displayed as the password button label

identifierPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the extension input

invalidIdentifierError.description: Error message for invalid user identifier

mirrorTooltipToRight.description: Whether the Login tooltip should open towards the right side of the screen

logInButtonBehavior.description: Defines the behavior of the login button when clicked

accountOptionsButtonBehavior.description: Defines the behavior of the account options button when clicked

accountOptionLinks.description: Creates custom links to replace the default ones set for accountOptionsButtonBehavior's link value

termsAndConditions.description: Text to be displayed below the login options regarding terms and conditions

hasGoogleOneTap.description: Whether Google's One-tap sign-up and auto sign-in solution should be enabled (Beta)

googleOneTapAlignment.description: Defines the alignment of the Google One-tap pop-up (Beta)

googleOneTapMarginTop.description: Defines the pop-up top margin for the Google One-tap login (Beta)

disabledEmailInputs.description: Whether user email editing should be allowed

blockOAuthAutoRedirect.description: If true and the only active provider is an OAuth, the login won't auto-redirect

^login(#.*)?$.description: Login block from vtex.login app — renders the login icon/button and tooltip with authentication options (email/password, access code, OAuth).

isInitialScreenOptionOnly.description: Whether only the login options will be displayed on the initial screen (true) or not (false).

defaultOption.description: Determines which login option is displayed initially: 0 = access-code, 1 = email/password, 2 = corporate.

optionsTitle.description: Text to entitle the login options.

emailAndPasswordTitle.description: Text to display the email-and-password option.

accessCodeTitle.description: Text to display the access-code option.

emailPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the email input.

passwordPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the password input.

showPasswordVerificationIntoTooltip.description: Whether a tooltip responsible for checking the password format should be shown.

acessCodePlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the access code input.

providerPasswordButtonLabel.description: Text to be displayed as the password button label.

identifierPlaceholder.description: Text to be displayed as placeholder to the extension input.

invalidIdentifierError.description: Error message for invalid user identifier.

termsAndConditions.description: Text to be displayed below the login options regarding terms and conditions.

disabledEmailInputs.description: Whether user email editing should be allowed (false = editing allowed).

^login-content(#.*)?$.description: Block that renders the login form content inline (not as a popover/modal), used for dedicated login pages.

classes.description: Overrides default CSS handles for React component usage.

^logo(#.*)?$.description: Displays the store logo as an image with optional hyperlink and label.

^manual-pricing(#.*)?$.description: Renders a manual price input for cart items, allowing overrides via a modal. Receives all data through React context (useItemContext) and has no configurable block-level props.

orientation.description: Defines whether the menu is rendered vertically or horizontally.

textType.description: Typography style applied to the menu items text.

items.description: Array of menu-item configurations, allowing items to be edited via Site Editor.

experimentalOptimizeRendering.description: Enables experimental rendering optimization for the menu.

categoryId.description: Category ID used to render a category-based menu automatically.

title.description: MenuItem schema used to render a title item at the top of the menu.

additionalDef.description: Defines an additional definition type for the menu (none, category, or title).

blockClass.description: Custom CSS class identifier used to override default CSS handles.

classes.description: Object used to apply custom CSS classes to specific menu CSS handles.

^menu(#.*)?$.description: Displays a navigation bar with links and dropdown submenus, supporting both category-based and custom menu items.

type.description: Menu item type, either category or custom.

id.description: Menu item identifier.

highlight.description: Indicates whether the item has a highlight.

iconPosition.description: Icon position relative to the menu item text, either to the left or right.

iconProps.description: Icon configuration props (id, isActive, size, viewBox, activeClassName, mutedClassName).

onMountBehavior.description: Indicates whether the submenu should automatically be displayed (open) or hidden (closed) when its parent is hovered/clicked.

itemProps.description: Item-specific configuration, either CategoryItem (categoryId, text) or CustomItem (type, href, noFollow, tagTitle, text).

blockClass.description: Used to override default CSS handles with custom class names.

^menu-item(#.*)?$.description: Renders a menu item that can be of type category or custom, supporting icons, submenus, and custom CSS handles.

links.description: Array of link objects to be displayed in the menu, each with text, route, and position configuration.

^menu-link(#.*)?$.description: Renders a list of navigation links in the store menu. Each link can point to an internal page or an external URL.

layout.description: Position of the availability message in the list: `rows` (in the product row) or `cols` (in the product column).

^message(#.*)?$.description: Renders an availability message for products in the product list, positioned according to the layout configuration.

drawerSlideDirection.description: Slide direction for the drawer Minicart opening.

linkVariationUrl.description: Link associated with the link Minicart variation.

maxDrawerWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels for the drawer Minicart when opened.

openOnHover.description: Indicates whether the popup Minicart should open when the user hovers over it.

quantityDisplay.description: Controls whether the quantity badge is shown even when the cart is empty.

itemCountMode.description: Determines how the item count in the cart badge is calculated.

backdropMode.description: Controls whether a backdrop is displayed when the drawer Minicart is opened.

customPixelEventId.description: Store event ID that triggers the minicart to automatically open.

customPixelEventName.description: Store event name that triggers the minicart to automatically open.

classes.description: Used to override default CSS handles via React component import (CustomCSSClasses).

^minicart(#.*)?$.description: Minicart block that displays a shopping cart icon with a drawer, popup, or inline cart view.

sideBarMode.description: Controls whether the content is rendered in sidebar (drawer) mode.

finishShoppingButtonLink.description: URL used by the checkout/finish-shopping button inside the minicart content.

classes.description: Custom CSS class overrides for the available CSS handles (e.g. minicartContentContainer, minicartProductListContainer, minicartTitle, minicartFooter).

^minicart-base-content(#.*)?$.description: Container block for the minicart's main content area, rendering the product list, summary, and empty state sections.

finishShoppingButtonLink.description: URL para a qual o usuário é redirecionado ao clicar no botão de checkout.

classes.description: Sobrescreve os CSS handles padrão do componente React (CustomCSSClasses).

^minicart-checkout-button(#.*)?$.description: Botão de finalizar compra exibido no rodapé do minicart. Redireciona o usuário para o checkout ao ser clicado.

classes.description: Overrides the default CSS handles classes. Use to apply custom CSS class names to the minicartEmptyStateContainer handle.

^minicart-empty-state(#.*)?$.description: Block displayed when the minicart cart is empty. Accepts children blocks for layout composition and supports CSS handle class overrides.

splitItem.description: Determines whether products with attachments should be split and treated as individual items (true) or as a single item (false).

^minicart-product-list(#.*)?$.description: Container block that renders the product list inside the Minicart drawer/popup. Its detailed configuration is documented in the vtex.product-list app.

classes.description: Map of custom CSS class names to override the block's CSS handles (e.g. minicartSummary).

^minicart-summary(#.*)?$.description: Container block that renders the order summary inside the minicart drawer. Its child block (checkout-summary.compact) handles totalizers and totals display. The block itself exposes only a CSS customization prop.

variation.description: Defines the minicart rendering behavior (popup, drawer, link, popupWithLink, or block).

drawerSlideDirection.description: Slide direction for the drawer when it opens.

linkVariationUrl.description: URL used when variation is set to 'link' or 'popupWithLink'.

maxDrawerWidth.description: Maximum width in pixels for the drawer when opened.

openOnHover.description: Whether the popup opens on hover instead of click.

quantityDisplay.description: Controls when the cart badge quantity counter is displayed.

itemCountMode.description: Defines how the item count in the badge is calculated.

backdropMode.description: Controls whether a backdrop overlay is displayed when the minicart is open.

MinicartIcon.description: Block used as the icon for the minicart button (e.g., from Store Icons app).

customPixelEventId.description: Store event ID that triggers the minicart to open automatically.

customPixelEventName.description: Store event name that triggers the minicart to open automatically (e.g., 'addToCart').

classes.description: Custom CSS class overrides for the block's CSS handles.

^minicart\\.v2(#.*)?$.description: Minicart v2 block — displays the shopping cart summary with configurable drawer, popup, or inline variations.

centered.description: Set to true if the modal component should be centered on screen.

closeOnEsc.description: Set to true if the modal should close when the Esc key is pressed.

closeOnOverlayClick.description: Set to true if the modal should close when clicking outside the modal area.

showCloseIcon.description: Set to true if the modal should display a close icon button.

buttonLabel.description: Label text displayed on the button that opens the modal.

buttonClass.description: Extra Tachyons classes to apply to the button container.

blockClass.description: Unique class name appended to the block's CSS classes for custom styling.

^modal(#.*)?$.description: Displays a button that, when clicked, opens a modal with its children blocks rendered inside. Deprecated in favor of vtex.modal-layout.

^modal-actions(#.*)?$.description: Logical block that only renders its child blocks, which in turn render call-to-action buttons inside the modal. Has no configurable props of its own.

label.description: Defines the text rendered in the close button. Default depends on the store's language (Cancel, Cancelar).

^modal-actions\\.close(#.*)?$.description: Button block that closes the modal when used inside modal-actions. Renders a close button with a configurable label.

^modal-content(#.*)?$.description: Defines how the modal content will be rendered through its children blocks. Preferably used alongside modal-header to create a modal with a header and footer.

showCloseButton.description: Defines whether the close icon button should be rendered in the modal header (true) or not (false).

iconCloseSize.description: Size (in pixels) of the close icon button in the modal header.

^modal-header(#.*)?$.description: Header section of a VTEX IO modal, optionally rendering a close button with configurable icon size.

scroll.description: Defines how users should scroll the modal.

blockClass.description: Unique block ID used for CSS customizations.

disableEscapeKeyDown.description: Defines whether the modal should be closed when pressing the Esc key.

fullScreen.description: Defines whether the modal should fill the whole screen (responsive).

backdrop.description: Defines whether the modal will have a clickable backdrop or none (responsive).

customPixelEventId.description: Store event ID that triggers the modal-layout block.

customPixelEventName.description: Store event name that triggers the modal-layout block.

^modal-layout(#.*)?$.description: Modal component that renders a modal dialog overlay, triggered by a trigger block or custom pixel events.

trigger.description: Defines whether the modal is triggered by user click, page load, or page load once per session.

customPixelEventId.description: Store event ID that triggers the modal-trigger block and opens the modal-layout blocks on the interface.

customPixelEventName.description: Store event name that triggers the modal-trigger block, e.g. 'addToCart' or 'removeFromCart'.

^modal-trigger(#.*)?$.description: Block that wraps the element which triggers the modal to open. Supports click, page load, or session-based load triggers.

blockDocument.description: Enables or disables editing the document field in MyAccount

^my-account(#.*)?$.description: Hub app that serves as entry point for store customers to manage personal data, addresses, orders, and credit cards.

defaultRoute.description: Default path rendered when the user opens the My Account URL (/account/). E.g. '/profile', '/orders', '/points'.

^my-account-defaultPage(#.*)?$.description: Block that defines the default home page of My Account, setting the path rendered when the user opens /account/.

render.description: Function that must be called with an array of objects containing `name` and `path` properties to create menu links. Required prop injected by the My Account extension point.

intl.description: Intl shape object injected via react-intl (intlShape) for internationalization of link labels.

^my-account-link(#.*)?$.description: Extension point block that allows apps to add custom links to the My Account menu. Components implementing this interface receive a render function and intl for i18n, and must call render with an array of {name, path} objects to create menu links.

label.description: Changes the section menu label on the My Account page.

^my-account-link\\.wishlist-link(#.*)?$.description: Link para a seção de Wishlist na página Minha Conta, permitindo customizar o rótulo exibido no menu.

^my-account-menu(#.*)?$.description: Container block that renders the My Account navigation menu with links to profile, addresses, orders, and cards. Has no documented first-level props — the block is purely structural and configured via child blocks (my-profile-link, my-addresses-link, my-orders-link, my-cards-link

^my-account-page(#.*)?$.description: Extension point block used to register custom pages inside the My Account section. It acts as a public extensibility slot (component: "*") and does not expose documented first-level props in the official VTEX documentation.

title.description: Title displayed in the My Account page header via ContentWrapper. If empty or not set, the page is rendered without the wrapper.

namespace.description: CSS namespace passed to the ContentWrapper component.

^my-account-page\\.wishlist-page(#.*)?$.description: Renders the Wishlist page inside the My Account section, optionally wrapping content in a titled ContentWrapper.

^my-account-pages(#.*)?$.description: Extension point block that acts as a container for My Account pages (my-orders, my-cards, and custom my-account-page blocks). Has no documented props of its own.

render.description: Callback function that receives an array of objects with `name` (display name) and `path` ('/addresses') to render the addresses menu link.

^my-addresses-link(#.*)?$.description: Link de navegação para a página de endereços do usuário no My Account. Segue o padrão de extensão de links do My Account, recebendo um prop render que deve ser chamado com um array de objetos contendo name e path.

^my-profile-link(#.*)?$.description: Block that renders a link to the My Profile page in the My Account menu. It uses the render prop pattern (injected by the framework) and has no user-configurable props documented in the official docs or source code.

label.description: Label text of the newsletter form.

placeholder.description: Placeholder text for the email input field.

submit.description: Label text for the submit button.

^newsletter(#.*)?$.description: Deprecated newsletter subscription form block. Displays a label, email input, and submit button. Replaced by the vtex.store-newsletter app.

checkboxLabel.default: I agree to received this newsletter.
checkboxLabel.description: Label for the confirmation checkbox. Supports ICU Message Format with `firstLink` and `secondLink` variables for interpolation.

firstLabelLink.description: Configures the `firstLink` variable to be interpolated by the text passed to `checkboxLabel`. Contains `url` and `text` fields.

secondLabelLink.description: Configures the `secondLink` variable to be interpolated by the text passed to `checkboxLabel`. Contains `url` and `text` fields.

classes.description: Used to override default CSS handles (CustomCSSClasses). React component only.

^newsletter-checkbox-confirmation(#.*)?$.description: Renders a confirmation checkbox for the newsletter subscription form, supporting customizable label text with interpolated links via ICU Message Format.

ErrorState.description: Block to be rendered if the form submission fails.

SuccessState.description: Block to be rendered if the form submission succeeds.

LoadingState.description: Block to be rendered while the form submission is loading. If not provided, a spinner is shown on the submit button.

classes.description: Custom CSS class names used to override default component styling. Only useful when importing the block as a React component.

^newsletter-form(#.*)?$.description: Top-level block that provides context and state management for the newsletter subscription form, wrapping child input and submit blocks.

dynamicFields.description: Hidden fields responsible for saving the user's custom data on Master Data once the newsletter form is submitted. Accepts bindingUrl and bindingId as values.

^newsletter-hidden-field(#.*)?$.description: Block that saves hidden user data (e.g. binding info) to Master Data when the newsletter form is submitted.

placeholderText.description: Placeholder text displayed inside the email input field.

inputLabel.description: Label text rendered above or beside the email input field.

errorMessage.description: Error message shown when the email entered is invalid.

classes.description: Overrides default CSS handles; useful when importing the block as a React component.

^newsletter-input-email(#.*)?$.description: Input field block for collecting email addresses in the newsletter subscription form.

placeholderText.description: Placeholder text for the name input (translated according to the store's locale).

errorMessage.description: Error message shown if the name input is empty (translated according to the store's locale).

classes.description: Used to override default CSS handles. Only useful when importing the block as a React component.

^newsletter-input-name(#.*)?$.description: Renders a name input field in the newsletter subscription form.

placeholderText.description: Placeholder text displayed inside the phone input field (locale-dependent).

inputLabel.description: Label text rendered above or alongside the phone input field.

errorMessage.description: Error message shown when the phone input is empty or invalid (locale-dependent).

classes.description: Overrides default CSS handles; useful when importing the block as a React component.

^newsletter-input-phone(#.*)?$.description: Renders a phone input field inside the newsletter form.

submitButtonLabel.description: Text displayed on the submit button.

classes.description: Used to override default CSS handles. Only useful when importing this block as a React component.

^newsletter-submit(#.*)?$.description: Submit button block for the newsletter form. Renders the button that submits the newsletter subscription form.

^not-found(#.*)?$.description: Block that renders a "not found" message when a search returns no products. It must be declared as a child of search-not-found-layout. The component has no configurable props exposed at the theme block level — content and search term are derived internally from SearchPageContext.

content.description: Text to be displayed in the notification bar.

classes.description: Allows overriding default CSS handles when using this block as a React component.

^notification(#.*)?$.description: Displays a notification bar with a text message across the top of the store page.

content.description: Text to be used in the notification bar.

classes.description: Enables overriding of default CSS handles for React component usage, following the CSS handles customization pattern.

^notification\\.bar(#.*)?$.description: Barra de notificação que exibe um texto configurável no topo ou em linha na loja.

content.description: Text to be displayed in the inline notification.

classes.description: Allows overriding default CSS handles; helpful when using this block as a React component.

^notification\\.inline(#.*)?$.description: Displays an inline notification text within the store page.

^op-bank-invoice-section(#.*)?$.description: Renders the bank invoice section if the payment method chosen was bank invoice. Has no configurable props — only CSS Handles for customization.

^op-confirmation-icon(#.*)?$.description: Renders the confirmation icon (checkmark) on the order placed page. This block has no configurable props.

enableGuestMessage.description: Flag that enables a different confirmation message (store/header.guest-email) for guest users.

^op-confirmation-message(#.*)?$.description: Renders the order confirmation message containing the client's email address, with optional alternate messaging for guest users.

^op-confirmation-title(#.*)?$.description: Renders the order confirmation title on the order placed page. This block has no configurable props.

^op-footer(#.*)?$.description: Defines the footer content of the Order Placed page. Accepts any child blocks but exposes no configurable props.

^op-header(#.*)?$.description: Defines the header content of the order-placed page. It is a container block that accepts any child blocks and exposes no configurable props.

^op-notices(#.*)?$.description: Renders a list of important informations relevant to the currently placed order. The messages may vary according to the type of order. This block accepts no configurable props.

^op-order(#.*)?$.description: Defines an order context to render its meta information and packages. Must wrap all order-specific child blocks. Accepts any children blocks but has no configurable props.

^op-order-customer(#.*)?$.description: Renders the customer information. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Has no configurable props.

^op-order-datetime(#.*)?$.description: Renders the date and time an order was placed. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Has no configurable props.

^op-order-delivery-packages(#.*)?$.description: Renders an order delivery packages information and product list. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Accepts no configurable props — all data is consumed from the useOrder() context hook.

^op-order-number(#.*)?$.description: Renders the order ID number. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Has no configurable props.

fullWidth.description: Make the options wrapper take full horizontal space.

myAccountPath.description: The path to redirect a user to their profile page (rendered by the vtex.my-account app).

^op-order-options(#.*)?$.description: Renders customer action options after an order is placed, such as links to the user's account or order details.

^op-order-payment(#.*)?$.description: Renders the customer payment method information. Must be placed inside an op-order block. No configurable props — only CSS handles (orderPaymentWrapper, orderPaymentItem) are available for customization.

^op-order-pickup-packages(#.*)?$.description: Renders order pickup packages information and product list. Must be placed inside an op-order block. No configurable props documented.

^op-order-seller(#.*)?$.description: Renders the seller of an order. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Accepts no configurable props.

^op-order-split-notice(#.*)?$.description: Renders a message with the number of packages of an order if the order was split in more than one package. Must be placed inside an op-order block. Accepts no props.

^op-order-takeaway-packages(#.*)?$.description: Renders the takeaway packages section of an order confirmation page, displaying takeaway parcels and their products. Receives no configurable props — all data is consumed from OrderContext via the useOrder() hook.

^op-order-total(#.*)?$.description: Renders the order total and pricing breakdown. Must be placed inside an op-order block. No configurable props — it reads totals directly from the order context.

^op-print-button(#.*)?$.description: Renders a button that triggers a full page print. Has no configurable props, no composition, and no CSS handles.

borderless.description: Remove a borda inferior da seção.

marginBottom.description: Espaço de margem posicionado abaixo da seção.

paddingBottom.description: Espaço de padding posicionado abaixo da seção.

name.description: Nome da seção. Utilizado para gerar CSS handles customizados para ela.

^op-section(#.*)?$.description: Container de seção reutilizável utilizado na página de pedido confirmado para agrupar blocos filhos com controle de bordas, margens, padding e largura.

^op-summary-section(#.*)?$.description: Renders a summary of all pickup and delivery packages (number of items, packages, pickup/delivery address and SLA) when a placed order is split between delivery and pickup fulfillment methods.
